commands.author: wangmiao0668000666
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=2e44a9d9bcd818a0915e2789a5b6832332f997be)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds an optional heartbeat `transcriptArtifactMode` config that can strip heartbeat artifacts, keep only a compact result, or keep the full heartbeat transcript.

PR surface: Source +94, Tests +133, Docs +13. Total +240 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: current code supports top-level `tool_calls`, and the PR head spreads the source assistant object while replacing only `content`, so the tool-call metadata survives. The linked user request itself is a feature request rather than a failing current-main bug.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can fix the blocking clean-summary defect; after that, maintainers still need to decide whether to accept the new config surface.

target.title: fix(image-generation): bound OpenAI-compatible image response reads
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`createOpenAiCompatibleImageGenerationProvider` parsed the success-path JSON body of OpenAI-compatible image generation/edit endpoints with an unbounded `await response.json()` at `src/image-generation/openai-compatible-image-provider.ts:272`. The sibling paths (`readProviderBinaryResponse`, `readResponseTextLimited`, `readProviderJsonResponse`) already cap reads at 16 MiB via `readResponseWithLimit`, so the image-generation success path was the one remaining unbounded reader in the image-generation capability — a malicious or malfunctioning provider streaming an endless JSON body could force the runtime to buffer the entire payload before failing.

This is the image-generation-endpoint counterpart to the recently merged bound-stream work in #95218 (provider JSON), #95417 (Google prompt cache), #95418 (OpenRouter model scan), #95420 (OpenRouter model capabilities), and #95246 (live model catalog), closing the same symmetric unbounded-read class on the image-generation path. Reuses the existing `readProviderJsonResponse` helper rather than introducing a new abstraction.

Behavior for well-formed and malformed JSON is unchanged; only the unbounded-size case now throws a clear overflow error instead of buffering without limit.

## Changes

- `src/image-generation/openai-compatible-image-provider.ts`: route the success-path JSON read through `readProviderJsonResponse<unknown>(response, failureLabel)` instead of `await response.json()`. The `failureLabel` is computed once and reused across `assertOkOrThrowHttpError` and `readProviderJsonResponse` so the bounded-reader overflow message and the HTTP error message stay aligned with the caller's `options.failureLabels`.
- `src/image-generation/openai-compatible-image-provider.test.ts`: route every mock through the new bounded reader; add a test that asserts the success body flows through `readProviderJsonResponse` with the right label, and a second test that asserts the bounded-reader overflow error surfaces verbatim and `release()` still runs.

## Evidence

- **Test**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/image-generation/openai-compatible-image-provider.test.ts --run` → `Test Files 1 passed (1) / Tests 7 passed (7)`.
- **Static checks**: `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/image-generation/openai-compatible-image-provider.ts src/image-generation/openai-compatible-image-provider.test.ts` — exit 0.
- **Direct read-path control**: the production code path now matches `readProviderJsonResponse`'s contract: stream body is read through `readResponseWithLimit` under a 16 MiB cap (`PROVIDER_JSON_RESPONSE_MAX_BYTES`), cancelling the stream on overflow instead of buffering it, then `JSON.parse` the bounded text. On overflow, throws `<failureLabel>: JSON response exceeds <n> bytes`; on malformed JSON, throws `<failureLabel>: malformed JSON response` — same error shape as every other `readProviderJsonResponse` caller in the codebase.
- **Backward compatibility**: callers using the existing `options.failureLabels` get the same string as the bounded-reader label, so error messages from the new path are easy to grep for. Callers without `failureLabels` get the existing default `${options.label} image generation failed` / `${options.label} image edit failed` strings.

## Out of scope

- The `mcp-http-fetch.ts`, `tools-manager.ts`, and `infra/clawhub.ts` unbounded reads are separate surfaces and tracked separately.
- `readProviderJsonResponse`'s 16 MiB cap is fixed; per-call `opts.maxBytes` overrides are not exposed by this PR.

## Risk

- Low: error message wording on the bounded-reader overflow path is new (`<failureLabel>: JSON response exceeds <n> bytes`). Existing call sites do not grep for this message and no test asserts it. Mitigated by reusing `readProviderJsonResponse`, which already produces the same wording for every other caller.
target.author: wangmiao0668000666
